steps.title: Identify the comparative claim
steps.content: The researchers' finding is comparative: bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with IWCI than riprap. To illustrate this, we need a finding showing the differential harm between the two types.

steps.title: Find the isolating comparison
steps.content:
Waterbird communities at Old Road, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of bulkheads, had lower average IWCI scores than did waterbird communities at Miles, a site with a relatively high percentage of shoreline consisting of riprap. directly compares a bulkhead-dominant site (Old Road) to a riprap-dominant site (Miles), showing Old Road has lower IWCI—perfectly illustrating that bulkheads harm community integrity more than riprap.
The others fail to isolate this comparison: A combines both types together, C is vague about percentages, and D doesn't show differential impact.
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
